== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県茂原市本納738', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tachibana-jinja (Mobara)}} [[JA:橘樹神社 (茂原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544302}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tachibana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tatibana jinja mobara haiden.JPG|280px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:上総国|社2]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:上総国の式内社]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:千葉県の旧県社]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:房総の魅力500選]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:弟橘媛|+]][[Category:qq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県茂原市本納738', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tachibana-jinja (Mobara)}} [[JA:橘樹神社 (茂原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544302}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tachibana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tatibana jinja mobara haiden.JPG|280px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '千葉県茂原市本納738', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tachibana-jinja (Mobara)}} [[JA:橘樹神社 (茂原市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11544302}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tachibana Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tatibana jinja mobara haiden.JPG|280px|]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tachibana Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} |神体 = Tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = (tradition) 40th year of the 12th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|lat_deg=35|lat_min=29|lat_sec=21.9|lon_deg=140|lon_min=18|lon_sec=20.7|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=260|coord={{coord|35|29|21.9|N|140|18|20.7|E}}}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (たちばなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Honno, {{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} and the {{ill|lt=Kazusa Province|Q1055394|WD=Q1055394|1=Kazusa Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. Its shrine crest is the "tachibana" citrus. In older records it was also written "Tachibana Jinja" and "Tachibaki Jinja." Today, the two characters "橘樹" are read "Tachibana." == Enshrined deities == '''Main deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|lt=Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}}''' (おとたちばなひめのみこと) '''Co-enshrined deities''' * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} (やまとたけるのみこと) - husband of Ototachibana-hime. * Oshiyama no Sukune (おしやまのすくね) - father of Ototachibana-hime. === History === === Overview === Shrine tradition says that during Yamato Takeru's eastern campaign, when crossing from {{ill|lt=Sagami Province|Q1053732|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami|1=Sagami Province}} to Kazusa and encountering a storm at sea, his consort {{ill|lt=Ototachibana-hime|Q11487396|WD=Q11487396|1=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto}} sacrificed herself to calm the seas. Yamato Takeru then made her tomb, enshrined her comb, and planted tachibana trees; this is said to be the origin. The ''{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' says "After seven days, her comb came ashore; they took it and made a tomb"[https://www.seisaku.bz/kojiki/kojiki_11.html Kojiki, Middle Book 4, Emperor Keiko], and this is identified with this shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', the related account of Yamato Takeru's sea crossing and onward route is also regarded as the origin narrative[https://www.seisaku.bz/nihonshoki/shoki_07.html Nihon Shoki, Vol.7, Emperor Keiko Chronicle]. The first appearance in the six national histories is in ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', entry for May 17, {{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}} ({{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1), granting Tachibaki deity Senior Fifth Rank Lower. On July 15, {{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}} (Gangyo 8), Senior Fifth Rank Upper was granted, and Yamato Takeru and Oshiyama no Sukune were co-enshrined. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears in Chōsei District of Kazusa Province as "Tachi Shrine," listed as a Shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine possessed shrine lands called Tachibaki-no-shō (later Ninomiya-no-shō). It is said to have been granted Senior First Rank in {{ill|lt=1681|Q7645|WD=Q7645|1=1681}} ({{ill|lt=Enpo|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 8). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} under the modern shrine ranking system. === Divine ranks === * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), May 17: from Junior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 to Senior Fifth Rank Lower Merit Grade 5 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Tachibaki deity". * {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 8 ({{ill|lt=884|Q23895|WD=Q23895|1=884}}), July 15: Senior Fifth Rank Upper Merit Grade 5 (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - recorded as "Tachi deity". === Precinct === The honden was built in {{ill|lt=1800|Q6875|WD=Q6875|1=1800}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 12), Edo period. Behind the honden remains a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} said to be the tomb of Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto. Before the honden was built, worship is said to have been directed from the haiden straight toward this tumulus. The Miyazuma Pond in the precinct is traditionally said to be a pit from digging that tomb. ファイル:Tatibana jinja mobara honden.JPG|Honden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 拝殿.JPG|Haiden ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 宮池.JPG|Miyazuma Pond ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 手水鉢.JPG|Temizu basin
{{ill|lt=Kan'en|Q1145723|WD=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}}). ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Sessha and massha === [[ファイル:橘樹神社 (茂原市) 摂末社.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sessha and massha]] All are massha. * Akiba Shrine ** Deities: Izanami-no-Mikoto, Homusubi-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Tensho era. * Kasushima Shrine ** Deity: Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Bunroku era. * Inari Shrine ** Deity: Ukemochi-no-Mikoto *: Enshrined since the foundation. * Koyasu Shrine ** Deity: Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto *: Invited in the Eiroku era. * Kureto Shrine ** Deity: Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto *: Relocated together with the fall of Honno Castle. === Main annual events === * New Year Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}})Festival list from shrine history record. * Genshi Festival ({{ill|lt=January 3|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}}) * Kinen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Great summer purification ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * Annual festival (day before {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Health and Sports Day|1=UNKNOWN}} in October) - formerly held on {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}}. * Niiname Festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Old talisman burning rite ({{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) * Great year-end purification ({{ill|lt=December 21|Q2603|WD=Q2603|1=December 21}}) === Cultural properties === === Chiba Prefecture designated cultural property === * Tachibaki-sha documents (with petition and complaint documents of Hasegawa Arinori) (calligraphy) - designated March 10, 1989. === Mobara City designated cultural properties === * Honno Tachibana Shrine grove (natural monument) - designated Feb 26, 1980. * Miyanoshita ruins (historic site) - designated Dec 28, 1978. * Tachibana Shrine Ogiu Sorai calligraphy plaque (with Sorai's autograph "Koto no Ki") and sacred cedar (calligraphy) - designated Jan 22, 1993. === Visitor information === '''Address''' * {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mobara|Q502486|WD=Q502486|1=Mobara}} Honno 738 '''Access''' * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Sotobo Line|Q670656|WD=Q670656|1=Sotobō Line}} {{ill|lt=Honno Station|Q564470|WD=Q564470|1=Honnō Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) * Nearest IC: {{ill|lt=Ken-o Expressway|Q5957728|WD=Q5957728|1=Ken-Ō Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Mobara-kita Interchange|Q2743550|WD=Q2743550|1=Mobara-kita interchange}} '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Honno Castle|Q11520739|WD=Q11520739|1=Honnō Castle}} ruins === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history record * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Chiba Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Mobara City section, Tachibana Shrine entry * Nakaaki Suzuki, "Tachi Shrine" (in {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed., ''Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, 11 Kanto'', {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, ISBN 4-560-02221-6) === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)|Q11544300|WD=Q11544300|lt=Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} (Kawasaki, Kanagawa) === External links === * [http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=5086 Tachibana Shrine] (Jinja no Hiroba, supervised by Chiba Prefecture Shrine Office) * [https://maruchiba.jp/spot/detail_10667.html Tachibana Shrine - Chiba Tourism Navi] official Chiba tourism site * [https://www.city.mobara.chiba.jp/promotion/0000000089.html Tachibana Shrine] Mobara City {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:茂原市の建築物]] [[Category:茂原市の歴史]] [[Category:Kazusa Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Top 500 Attractions of Bōsō Peninsula]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]][[Category:qq]]
